LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT UNIVERSAL MANUFACTURING CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. THE "COMPLETED AND ACCEPTED DOCTRINE" APPLIES WHERE THE DEFECT IS PATENT. SEE SANCHEZ V. SWINERTON & WALBERG (1996) 47 CAL.APP.4TH 1461, 1470. IN THIS CASE, DEFENDANT PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE REGARDING THE STEP, WHAT THE DEFECT WAS, AND WHETHER THE DEFECT WAS PATENT OR LATENT. IN ADDITION, EX. D TO THE DECLARATION OF DAVID HART CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT RETAINED CONTROL OVER THE TOWER AND/OR WHETHER AMERICAN BRIDGE ACCEPTED DEFENDANT'S WORK. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-15-2014 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-17-2014 Continued To Jan-12-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Ntc
Off Calendar May-05-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew D. Barnette, Esq. of Archer Norris, Tel. 925.930.6600, for defendant and moving party;
David L. Hart, Esq. of the Law office of David Hart, Tel. 650.355.3000, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT UNIVERSAL MANUFACTURING CORPORATION'S Motion For Summary Judgment is denied because defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. The "completed and accepted doctrine" applies where the defect is patent. See Sanchez v. Swinerton & Walberg (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 1461, 1470. In this case, defendant presents no evidence regarding the step, what the defect was, and whether the defect was patent or latent. In addition, Ex. D to the declaration of David Hart creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant retained control over the tower and/or whether American Bridge accepted defendant's work. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Continued To Aug-11-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Ntc
Off Calendar Apr-03-2014 Continued To May-05-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
